market_context: US stock futures are edging higher in pre-market trading, with Dow futures up 0.5-0.6%, S&P 500 futures up 0.5-0.6%, and Nasdaq-100 futures up 0.4-0.6%, driven by hopes of progress in US-Iran talks amid surging oil prices (Brent above $115/bbl, WTI above $101/bbl).[1][2] Energy and materials sectors like mining stocks (Alcoa +10%, Century Aluminum +11%) are outperforming, while tech names show pre-market buying interest after recent declines; no major economic data today in a holiday-shortened week closing for Good Friday.[1][2]

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures are up ~0.6% pre-market amid positive Iran talk signals from Trump, with prediction markets showing >6400 at 56¢ probability on Robinhood.[1][2][4] Oil surge supports energy rotation but modest broad gains expected in holiday-shortened week without major data.[1]

Nasdaq-100 futures rose 0.4% pre-market with buying in tech heavyweights like Nvidia, Amazon, Microsoft after recent correction deepened last week.[1][2] Geopolitical optimism offsets oil-driven volatility, though Nasdaq lags slightly behind Dow/S&P futures.[1]

Energy sector outperforms on Brent >$115 (+2%) and WTI >$101 amid Iran tensions, with sector rotation evident; Trump's comments on Iran oil shipments add tailwind.[1][2] Pre-market strength in related miners like Alcoa (+10%) supports continued gains.[2]
Dow futures lead with +280-300 points (0.6%) pre-market on Iran deal hopes balancing oil surge risks.[1][2] Holiday week and lack of data favor modest rebound from last week's volatility.[1]
